articles.title: OpenAI Launches GPT-5 with Native Multimodal Reasoning and Extended Context
articles.summary: OpenAI officially released GPT-5, featuring deeply integrated multimodal reasoning across text, image, audio, and video inputs with a context window exceeding 1 million tokens. The model demonstrates significant improvements in long-horizon planning, mathematical proof generation, and agentic tool use, narrowing the gap on several AGI benchmarks. Enterprise API access is rolling out with new structured-output guarantees and latency improvements.

articles.title: Google DeepMind's Gemini Robotics Models Enable Zero-Shot Dexterous Manipulation
articles.summary: DeepMind unveiled Gemini Robotics, a family of vision-language-action models that allow robotic arms to perform complex dexterous manipulation tasks with zero-shot generalization to novel objects. The system combines Gemini's world-knowledge with real-time proprioceptive feedback loops, achieving human-comparable success rates on 47 of 52 benchmark tasks. Several hardware partners including Apptronik and Agility Robotics are integrating the models into commercial platforms.

articles.title: Figure AI Raises $1.5B Series C, Begins Warehouse Deployments with BMW
articles.summary: Figure AI closed a $1.5 billion Series C at an $18 billion valuation, fueled by production deployments of its Figure 02 humanoid robot at BMW's Spartanburg manufacturing facility. The robots are autonomously performing bin picking, parts kitting, and inter-station transport across three production lines. Figure reports sub-2-second grasp planning and 99.4% pick reliability in production conditions, a milestone for general-purpose humanoid deployment.

articles.title: Anthropic Introduces Claude Agent SDK with Built-In Safety Scaffolding
articles.summary: Anthropic released an open-source Agent SDK designed to let developers build autonomous Claude-powered agents with built-in Constitutional AI guardrails, sandboxed code execution, and human-in-the-loop checkpoints. The framework supports multi-step tool chains, persistent memory, and real-time monitoring dashboards. Early adopters report cutting agent development time by 60% compared to custom LangChain-style pipelines while maintaining tighter safety controls.

articles.title: TSMC Begins Volume Production of 1.4nm Gate-All-Around Chips
articles.summary: TSMC announced volume production on its N14G (1.4nm-class) gate-all-around nanosheet process at its Kaohsiung fab, with Apple and Nvidia confirmed as launch customers. The node delivers a 20% speed improvement and 30% power reduction over N2, critical for next-generation AI accelerator and mobile SoC designs. Yield rates reportedly exceeded expectations, easing concerns about the economic viability of sub-2nm manufacturing.

articles.title: Meta Open-Sources Llama 4 Behemoth with Mixture-of-Experts Topping Benchmarks
articles.summary: Meta released Llama 4 Behemoth, a 2-trillion-parameter mixture-of-experts model with 256 experts and 37B active parameters per forward pass, under a permissive open license. The model tops GPT-5 on several academic reasoning and coding benchmarks while remaining deployable on a single 8×H200 node. The release intensifies the open-weight vs. proprietary debate and gives the open-source ecosystem its most capable foundation model to date.

articles.title: Boston Dynamics and Toyota Research Demonstrate Collaborative Multi-Robot Construction
articles.summary: Boston Dynamics and Toyota Research Institute demonstrated a team of Atlas electric humanoids and Spot quadrupeds autonomously constructing a modular emergency shelter in under four hours. The system uses a shared spatial-semantic map and decentralized task allocation, with robots dynamically reassigning roles when one encounters an obstacle. The demonstration targets disaster-response and defense logistics applications with field trials planned for Q3 2026.

articles.title: Helion Energy Achieves Sustained Fusion Plasma Milestone, Signs Second Commercial PPA
articles.summary: Helion Energy reported sustaining a 100-million-degree plasma for over 20 seconds in its seventh-generation Polaris prototype, a record for a private fusion company using field-reversed configuration. The company simultaneously announced a second commercial power purchase agreement, this time with Nucor Steel, following its earlier Microsoft deal. Helion targets first electricity delivery in late 2027, positioning pulsed fusion as a viable pathway to abundant clean energy for AI data center demand.
